content.full_text:
RAvI MERTA
CHM RMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES
COMMISSION
July 9, 1997
M. Trista Campbell
City ofBaldwin Park
14403 East Pacific Avenue
Baldwin Park, California 91706
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-97-188
Dear Ms. Campbell:
This letter is a response to your request for advice regarding the mass mailing provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’
QUESTION
Do the mass mailing provisions prohibit an article in the Baldwin ParkNOWabout
Mr. Ni’ifinez, a director of a city department, who is also an elected member of the school board?
CONCLUSION
Yes. Since Mr. Nifinez is affiliated with one of the agencies that produces and sends the
mass mailing, the mass mailing provisions prohibit any article that “features” Mr. Ninez.
FACTS
You edit the city section of a newspaper, the Baldwin Park NOW. The paper is funded
and published by the City of Baldwin Park, the Baldwin Park Chamber of Commerce and the
Baldwin Park Unified School District. The free paper is sent unsolicited to all residents ofthe
City of Baldwin Park.
Mr. Ralph Nüfinez is the Director of Recreation and Community Services for the City of
Baldwin Park and is an elected member on the Baldwin Park Unified School District Board
(“board”). You would like to write an article for the newspaper about Mr. Nüfinez in his capacity
as the Director of Recreation and Community Services.
Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
428 1 STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No. A-97-188
Page 2
ANALYSIS
Section 89001, as implemented by Regulation 18901 (copy enclosed), prohibits a mass
mailing
2 sent at public expense if all ofthe following criteria apply:
“(1) Any item sent is delivered, by any means, to the recipient at
his or her residence, place of employment or business, or post
office box. For purposes of this subdivision (a)(1), the item
delivered to the recipient must be a tangible item, such as a
videotape, record, or button, or a written document.
(2) The item sent either:
(A) Features an elected officer affiliated with the agency which
produces or sends the mailing, or
(B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or other reference to an
elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends
the mailing, and is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation,
coordination, or concert with the elected officer.
(3)(A) Any of the costs of distribution is paid for with public
moneys; or
(B) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding $50.00 are
paid with public moneys, and the design, production, or printing is
done with the intent of sending the item other than as permitted by
this regulation.
(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items are sent, in
a single calendar month, excluding any item sent in response to an
unsolicited request and any item described in subdivision (b).”
(Regulation 18901(a).)
Subdivision (c) of the regulation gives the following definitions:
“(1) Elected officer affiliated with the agency means an elected officer
who is a member, officer, or employee of the agency, or of a subunit thereof such
as a committee, or who has supervisory control over the agency, or who appoints
one or more members of the agency.
(2) Features an elected officer means the item mailed includes the elected
officer’s photograph or signature, or singles out the elected officer by the manner
of display of his or her name or office in the layout ofthe document, such as by
headlines, captions, type size, typeface, or type color.”
2 Section 82041.5 and Regulation 18901(a)(4) defme “mass mailing” for purposes of Section 89001 as
over 200 unsolicited and substantially similar items sent in a calendar month.


File No. A-97-188
Page 3
Mr. Nüfinez is the Director of Recreation and Community Services for the City of
Baldwin Park and is also an elected member of the school board. You would like to write an
article in the Baldwin ParkNOW about Mr. Nüfinez in his capacity as the director of the city
recreation department. However, he is also an elected school board member and, as such, is
affiliated with one of the agencies (the school district) which is producing and sending the mass
mailing. Since NOW is a publication funded by the City ofBaldwin, the Baldwin Park Chamber
of Commerce and the Baldwin Park Unified School District, the newspaper article may not
“feature” Mr. Nüfnez, absent an exception.
Please note that Regulation 18901(b)(1) exempts a roster listing containing all the names
of the elected officers of the agency. A roster listing may be printed in the newspaper ifthe
specific requirements in the regulation are followed. (Denhalter Advice Letter, No. 1-95-135,
copy enclosed.)
I trust this answers your question. If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
/114Wt
By:
Jil” techfr
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
SGC:JS :ak
Enclosures

sections.question:
Do the mass mailing provisions prohibit an article in the Baldwin ParkNOWabout
Mr. Ni’ifinez, a director of a city department, who is also an elected member of the school board?
sections.conclusion:
Yes. Since Mr. Nifinez is affiliated with one of the agencies that produces and sends the
mass mailing, the mass mailing provisions prohibit any article that “features” Mr. Ninez.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Do the mass mailing provisions prohibit an article in the Baldwin ParkNOWabout
Mr. Ni’ifinez, a director of a city department, who is also an elected member of the school board?

CONCLUSION: Yes. Since Mr. Nifinez is affiliated with one of the agencies that produces and sends the
mass mailing, the mass mailing provisions prohibit any article that “features” Mr. Ninez.
